text:
Once upon a time in a tiny hamlet nestled among rolling hills, there lived a quiet girl named Willow. She liked birds. Every morning she'd watch robin chicks learn how to fly outside her bedroom window. Whenever she imagined herself as one of those birds soaring, she'd hop onto her bike and pedal.

Willow knew her grandfather had built trees. Every Halloween, he'd decorate them like characters from fairy tales. So one year, Willow asked him to build her a bird tree. It stood eight feet tall when finally complete.

Every evening Owl Whisperer she'd listen to her favorite tunes. In the dreams, she'd glide like she learned from those songs. One night her wings caught fire. Bad dream.

Spring came. Birds sang. Willow decided she'd write stories about her adventures. They'd be forever. Willow sketched. Her stories were poems inside drawings.

Birds flew. Willow flew. Friends came. Just don't flap her head. Willow dreamed. Fly.<|endoftext|><|pad|>
